etymology.description: Depicts two figures face-to-face, fighting with bare hands
etymology.interpretation: The traditional character 鬥(dòu) represents two figures opposed, illustrating the concept of contest or struggle. It vividly depicts the idea of confrontation, which is embedded in its historical usage and imagery.
etymology.simplification: The simplified character 斗(dòu) retained the basic outline and dropped one of the components, simplifying the strokes while preserving the original meaning even though it was derived from variant forms.

examples.chinese: 他用斗舀水(他用斗舀水)。
examples.english: He uses a ladle to scoop water.

examples.chinese: 这个斗很旧(這個斗很舊)，我们需要买一个新的(我們需要買一個新的)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè gè dǒu hěn jiù, wǒmen xūyào mǎi yī gè xīn de.
examples.english: This ladle is very old, we need to buy a new one.
